PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
HOW DO I TRANSITION FROM TEACHING
TO MAKING AN OFFER FOR MY
PRODUCT ?
Every webinar element is vital, but the pivot (in other words, pivoting from
content mode to sales mode) is right up there close to the top. Too abrupt,
you lose people in droves. Psychologically you want them to feel:
• 	Grateful for the content (reciprocity)
• 	Commitment to moving forward
• 	Inadequate to take the next steps themselves
• 	Desire to continue to get more (FOMO)
• 	Positive momentum to move forward
THE PIVOT
There are 6 steps to nailing the pivot:
✓ 	Review what you’ve covered
✓ 	Commitment to moving forward
✓ 	“But you still need more…”
✓ 	“At this point you have a choice…”
✓ 	“So you can achieve…”
✓ 	Ask a series of “yes” questions
The goal is to get 4-6 “yeses” in 15 seconds… This is rapid fire, don’t wait for
responses. Then…
✓ 	Announce your product name (“Then it is with great pleasure that I
introduce you to (your product name”))
PIVOT TIMELINE
This part of your webinar should last approximately 3 to 5 minutes if you’re
planning a 60 minute webinar and 4 to 6 minutes if you’re planning a 90
minute webinar.
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	3 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
6 WAYS TO BLOW YOUR PIVOT
• 	Skip it (don’t just jump right into the sale)
• 	Change your tone of voice to be apologetic (be bold, you’ve just earned
the right to sell to them!)
• 	Be too bold (confidently assertive is the perfect tone)
• 	Do a “how did I do?” poll (instead, just tell them what you gave them)
• 	Rush through it (each of the 6 steps above are vital)
• 	Say something that implies that the important part is now over (the most
important part of the webinar is just beginning for you and for them)
For more on this topic, see the
Webinar Pivot Checklist

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
WHAT ARE ALL THE STEPS TO THE
OFFER?
This is one of the places where many people get concerned. They don’t want
to do webinars because they don’t want to come across as “salesy” or “pitchy.”
Rest assured… there’s a formula. And all you have to do is follow it.
THE OFFER PROCESS OVERVIEW
STEP #1. PRODUCT AND MISSION
Following up from the pivot, now is the time to reveal the name of your
product and give your prospects a strong call to action.
✓ 	This is where you say, “Then it is with great pleasure that I introduce you
to (product name).”
✓ 	At this point, put your logo on the screen
✓ 	Then state your Product statement, immediately followed by your
Mission statement
✓ 	Product statement
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	5 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
• 	“(Your product name) is a(n) (coaching program, self-paced
online training program/personal consulting program) that/
where we/where I personally (what your program helps
them accomplish).”
• 	DO NOT SAY, “What I’ll teach you” or “What you’ll learn.”
✓ 	Mission statement
• 	“Your mission, should you choose to accept it, is to do (what
they’ll accomplish) within the next (time period) so you can
(result) and (end result).”
What is your product statement?

STEP #2. BENEFITS
Features are what the product is/what’s included in it. Features are logical,
convincing, and used to justify the purchase once prospects have emotionally
decided to buy. Benefits are what the features do for your prospects. Benefits
are more emotional; they are motivating, and they help make the sale. Both
are necessary, but don’t get the priority wrong (75% of time spent on this
should be on the benefits and 25% should be on the features).
✓ 	Always show the benefits before you describe the features
✓ 	Save a few benefits for your close
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	6 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
✓ 	Use this simple formula for communicating features and benefits:
“(Feature) so that (benefit).” For example: “Module 1 shows you how to get
off email spammer lists so that your email gets delivered.”
What are the benefits of your program?
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________

______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
STEP #3. SECOND-LEVEL BENEFITS
Second-level benefits take your features and benefits a step further.
These are the changes in their lives as a result of the benefits. In other
words, second-level benefits describe what the benefits mean to them; what
changes in their lives as a result of getting the benefits. These are usually
high-level transformations such as time, freedom, impact, love, etc.
✓ 	Use this simple formula for communicating benefits and second-level
benefits: State benefits (“Here’s what you get”), followed by… State
second-level benefits (“Here’s what that means to you”). Here’s an
example that includes features, benefits and second-level benefits:
“Module 1 shows you how to get off email spammer lists so that your email
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	7 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
gets delivered. What that means is you will make more money in your
business."
What are the second-level benefits of your program?
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________

______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
STEP #4. FEATURES
The features are the “what” of your product. For example: 5 modules,
recordings, videos, course portal, quizzes, evergreen access, etc., (don’t make
the # of hours in your program a feature). Your objective is to give them just
enough information to justify the emotional decision they are making to buy…
They watch your webinar, and they’re emotionally sold on buying, now they
have to explain and justify that purchase to others, and to themselves when
they start getting second thoughts… What are they going to say to that
person? That’s your feature list.
✓ 	Briefly describe the features. It should never take more than 5 minutes, ½
that if possible
✓ 	Remember the key is not what’s in each module/element, it’s what they’ll
do/accomplish as a result of it
What are the features of your program?

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
STEP #5. PRICE ANCHORING
Price anchoring is the process of creating a foundational price against
which your price will be perceived as being a discount. The number needs
to be at least twice to ten times what you’re charging for the product. You
should price anchor:
• 	Against what they would have to pay to hire you to do this for them
• 	Or the rate you would have to charge them to train them one-on-one
• 	Or the cost to figure it out themselves
Another powerful price anchor is to assign a value to each module then do a
“value stack” where you list each module and it’s value.
How will you anchor the price of your program?
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
STEP #6 AND #7: PRICE
Now is the time to reveal your price. Once you've anchored your price, you
want to communicate:
✓ 	“I’m not going to charge you that if you invest on this webinar…”
✓ 	“Because______ (the “because” gives you a logical reason for discounting
this for them). Examples:
• 	They’re you’re loyal clients who have worked with you in the past
• 	“Though I’ve taught this to others in the past, this will be the first time
I’ve ever taught in this format.”
• 	“This is the first time I’ve ever taught this material, so I’m giving you a
special price in exchange for your frank feedback and hopefully a
testimonial at the end.”
• 	“You’ve made the effort to attend and learn on this webinar, so clearly
you’re committed to improving your life.”

• 	“I’m convinced this is the fastest, best way to _______ and I’m looking to
get this message out to the world.”
✓ 	“So I’m giving it to you, for a limited time, for just $_____.”
What will the price of your program be?

STEP #8 AND #9. BONUSES AND OFFER STACK
Now that you’ve revealed the price of your product, you want to sweeten the
deal by stating your bonuses and offer stack. A great way to do this is to turn
it into a story. When you do this right, you can spend more time talking about
your bonuses, and their value, than about the product itself. That’s the goal!
One thing to consider is taking elements out of the main product and
making them part of the bonuses (because people tend to place even more
value and attention on the bonuses than to the base product when they’re
buying).
✓ 	State your first bonus. You want to communicate:
“As I was putting this webinar together, I looked at the incredible value that
this program offers, giving you x, y, and z. And I got incredibly excited,
thinking about the amazing changes that those who choose to invest in it
would be making in their lives. But, then I had a thought. If someone had just
invested in this amazing program, and had x, y and z, what else would they
need? And I realized that though this program taught x, y, and z, they would
get even better results if they knew/understood/did/had________. So, I thought
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	10 	21
bonus tip!
when revealing your price, avoid using the words
“price” or “cost” and use “investment” instead
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

‘why not give them that too?’ So I put together this amazing training that
gives you _____.”
✓ 	Then, describe that bonus, give it a value and add it to a value-stack slide
✓ 	Add each bonus to the slide as you go along. You want to communicate:
“But, then I had a thought. If someone had just invested in this amazing
program, and had x, y and z, AND this incredible bonus that gives them______,
what else would help them to achieve their goal of ____________. And I realized
that I already have another program that I’ve created that helps them to do
________. You can buy that program on my website right now for $______, but
hey I’m in the mood to give you a great deal today, so, I’m going to throw it in
as another free bonus.”
✓ 	And then the next bonus. You want to communicate:
“If someone had just invested in this amazing program, and had x, y and z,
and this incredible bonus that gives them ______, PLUS this new bonus that
gives them____what else would they need to get them to success faster?”

STEP #10. BENEFITS AND PROOF
At this point you’ve been logically selling. It’s time to get emotion back in. The
easiest way to do that is to restate the benefits you offer and provide some
proof, all of that leading to the second-level benefits they’ll receive. By doing
this, psychologically you’re taking your prospects from logical mode to
emotional mode, and converted their thought process from, “Is the stuff I’m
being promised enough to justify the price?” to “Yes, this transformation is
what I want in my life!”
✓ 	Restate benefits (from the program, and from the bonuses) and proof.
Then, reinforce those emotions with case studies / testimonials. You want
to communicate:
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	11 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
“And here’s what you get when you invest in (your program name)” plus (the
benefits of the bonuses) giving you (the second-level benefits they’ll receive
as a result of those) like ______, who __________. And ________, who _________.
And to make sure that this works for you, specifically, I’m proud to announce
my (name of your guarantee).”

______________________________________________________________________________
STEP #11. GUARANTEE
Guarantees are purely emotional. They reduce the risk of buying by
attempting to resolve the question of “what if this isn’t right for me?” There
are 5 types of guarantees:
1. 	The “X-day money-back-for-any-reason” guarantee
2. 	The conditional money-back guarantee. This adds conditions to the
requirements. “If you x and still want your money back, we’ll give it to you,
but you have to (for example) either:
• 	“Prove that you executed our system and it didn’t work for you”
• 	“Prove that you executed our system and didn’t make at least
$1000 from it”
• 	“Create at least 10 videos using our formula and posted them to
YouTube”
3. 	The “earn back as much as you paid” guarantee. This is where you
guarantee they’ll achieve “x” or their money back. For example:
• 	“Earn at least $2k within 10 weeks” (or guarantee that you’ll make
up the difference)
• 	Set conditions they have to abide by (these have to be clear in
advance, and throughout the process)
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	12 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
4. 	The “we’ll work with you until you succeed” guarantee. This doesn’t
give their money back, but promises support until they do succeed
These (above) are all called risk-reversal guarantees and will increase sales.
The longer the guarantee, the more effective. Most people do a standard 30-
day money back guarantee, and spend 60 seconds talking about it. Only go
longer if your offer is unique.
5. 	The no-guarantee option. You can only pull off a no-guarantee if you’ve
built incredible trust. You want to communicate:
“I’m not offering a guarantee on this because I’ve found that too many people
use a guarantee as an easy-out when things get hard. So my giving you a
guarantee will actually hurt you. Buy this because you’re serious about______.
Then let’s work together to get you there!”
What will your guarantee be?

______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
STEP #12. FAST-ACTION BONUSES
A fast-action bonus is a very time-limited bonus that expires at the end of
the webinar to push them to make a decision. This is one of the best ways
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	13 	21
your guarantee
most people do a standard 30-day money back
guarantee, and spend 60 second talking about it in
their webinar (only go longer if your offer is unique)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
to get people to buy. You can extend a fast-action bonus to the end of the day
but best results come from end-of-webinar expiration. The best types of fast-
action bonuses are added contact with you (group or individual coaching,
especially if you can upsell them on that into additional coaching), or
something they really, really want badly enough to stop thinking and take
action. Here’s how you do it:
✓ 	Announce the bonus
✓ 	Tell attendees this section will not be in the replay
✓ 	Give them the opportunity to ask questions about the bonus before
continuing the webinar
✓ 	Promise you’ll send an email to confirm they’ve earned it
✓ 	Remove it from the replay
✓ 	Mention it one more time at the very end (giving them x more minutes)
✓ 	At the end of expiration time
✓ 	Tag everyone who has ordered during the time period
✓ 	Send them an email (prewritten) confirming that they received
the bonus
Will you use a fast-action bonus? If so, what will it be?

______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
STEP #13. SHOW THE URL
✓ 	Now it’s time to let people buy! You want to communicate:
“I’m proud to announce that (your product name) is officially available. Simply
go to the URL on your screen to get (the transformation your program
provides).”
✓ 	The URL stays on the screen from this point onwards, always in the same
place, always in the same color, font, etc.
✓ 	Some tools let you have a clickable button. Use it! And put the URL on the
screen too
✓ 	Communicate your deadline. You want to communicate:
“But this deal expires on (date/time). After which (something bad happens)”

Your Product Url Checklist
✓ 	Simple
✓ 	Only one
✓ 	Add something besides your product name (“get”, “buy”, “now”)
✓ 	Don’t put tracking codes in
✓ 	Short (don’t use bitly)
✓ 	You may want to use camel case
(CapitalizeTheFirstLetterOfEachWord.com)
✓ 	You may want to use a separate url for webinars
When someone goes to the URL:
✓ 	Abbreviated sales page, summarizing the offer
✓ 	Offer only (list the elements in the offer itself: product, bonuses,
guarantee, price, buy button), or
✓ 	Offer and sales elements (everything in the offer only, plus brief
positioning, testimonials, benefits, FAQ, how your life will be different)
✓ 	Do NOT include a video on the abbreviated sales page

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
STEP #14. “THIS IS RIGHT FOR YOU IF…”
At this point, you need to resolve the question “Is this right for me?” You want
to communicate:
✓ 	“This is right for you if…”
• 	Criteria 1
• 	Criteria 2
• 	Criteria 3
• 	etc
✓ 	“This is not right for you if…”
• 	Criteria 1
• 	Criteria 2
• 	Criteria 3
• 	etc
✓ 	Some combination of the two… Driving the wrong people away, while
getting people’s pride up so they want to prove you wrong
Creating your criteria:
• 	Some criteria are literal (“You’re in a serious relationship with someone.”).
These deal with whether the product is a fit for them
• 	Some are motivational (“You’re an action-taker.”). These are designed to
get them thinking, “You’re absolutely right I’m that kind of person (how
dare you think otherwise!)”

STEP #15, #16 AND #17. TAKING QUESTIONS
Now is the time in the webinar to start taking questions.
✓ 	Offer to take questions. You want to communicate:
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	16 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
“Now I’m going to open things up for questions, so I invite you to write your
questions in the chat. Let’s focus first on questions about (your product
name) and the offer I just made. Does anyone have any questions about
them? While you write those in, let me answer a few of the questions that
may be on your mind right now/or that others have asked.”
✓ 	Then answer a series of questions specifically designed to show your
product/offer in the best light. For example, questions about:
• 	What’s in the product itself, allowing you to remind them about the
features of your product
• 	Your guarantee so you can restate it
• 	Your cart-close deadline (NOT your fast-action deadline) so you can
emphasize the importance of ordering now
• 	What kind of results they may achieve, allowing you to showcase a
couple of case studies/testimonials
• 	The content of your bonuses so you can re-stress those offerings
At this point, you’re only talking about your product and offer. Avoid dropping
back into training mode, with one exception:
✓ 	Choose one answer that enables you to teach again for 1-2 minutes, then
tie that back to one of the modules/features of your program. You want to
communicate:
“Oh, there’s one other thing I wanted to tell you about in this webinar. (Teach
it). We cover this in detail in Module 3.”
✓ 	Couch all your questions and answers in a way that will encourage them
to buy
✓ 	Never go more than 2 questions without inviting them to visit the URL on
their screen
✓ 	Always keep these slides handy:
• 	Benefits slide
• 	Offer/bonus stack slide
• 	What’s in the product slide
• 	Product hero picture with the price on it
• 	All with your urls on them
• 	Show them often, point out what they get for investing
✓ 	Then, state how long the webinar has gone on for. You want to
communicate:
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	17 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
“Now, it’s already been (state how long the webinar’s gone on for), we’ve got
just a few more minutes together, and I’m happy to answer all the other
questions I can.”
Your Guide to Answering Questions
✓ 	Briefly answer their question as quickly as possible
✓ 	Tell them this is answered in depth in your program (if it’s not in your
program, point out how people can get questions answered during your
program (group calls, forum, comments section) and tell them it will be
covered in depth there)
✓ 	Never leave them totally satiated with your answer, but don’t leave them
totally frustrated either
✓ 	Sometimes you need to remind them of what they don’t know: “I just told
you x, and that’s really important, but if you try to do this without also
understanding y and z, horrible things will happen to you…”
✓ 	Don’t forget to be: Nice, patient, helpful (all of these help increase sales)
✓ 	Have a separate screen open, showing sales. Regularly pause what you’re
doing to welcome in new buyers, one-by-one, by name
✓ 	Don’t forget to restate your offer every 5 to 10 minutes
✓ 	Keep answering questions as long as they’re still buying (it’s not unusual
to go an hour)

STEP #19 AND #20. CLOSING YOUR WEBINAR
The best webinars don’t just end, they’re consciously finished. This is what
you want people thinking at the end of the webinar: "This has been amazing.
I’d love to have more. I want to do/be more. (Your name) is the person who will
help get me there (all this whether they buy or not)." And remember, they
may not buy today, but they may buy tomorrow, or next year.
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	18 	21
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 21 --

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
This is how to create these emotions:
✓ 	Summarize what you’ve covered (less than 2 minutes)
✓ 	Summarize the case studies/testimonials/proof you’ve offered or provide
additional proof they haven’t heard yet (exhortations to buy from those
who offered the proof) (2 minutes). Save up “a humdinger” proof element
for this part (and use it again in the emails)
Gap-Creating Psychology Script
✓ 	“I suspect because you’ve been on this call all the way up to this point that
this is important to you. And I know that the thought’s probably gone
through your mind that, ‘This looks great, but I don’t have the money to
invest in this right now. I’ll invest next time this offer comes around.’”
✓ 	State where will they be a year from now: “You’ve probably been working
on this for months, even years. How much progress have you made on it
during that time? What will be different in your life a year from now? My
guess is, nothing. Money will still be tight, you’ll still have bills to pay, you
still won’t have time to do it…”
✓ 	Give them an alternative: “Let’s imagine that you invest today. Where will
you then be a year from now? Picture yourself, having everything this
program offers during the year. Can you see what changes you will have
made in your life during that time?”
✓ 	Talk through examples of the things they would have done
✓ 	“And ask yourself the question, ‘Will my life be better a year from now if I
invest today or not?’ To me, the answer’s clear. Because I’ve been where
you are, and I am where I am, and I know for a fact that my life is better on
this end than it is where you’re sitting right now. I have a vision for your
future, and invite you to join me in that vision. I invite you to go to that URL
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	19 	21
remember
they may not buy today, but they may buy
tomorrow, or next year
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M5. WEBINAR OFFER PLANNER
right now and invest in creating your future. A future of (state second-level
benefits).”
✓ 	It’s better to not do it alone: “And I know that the thought’s probably gone
through your mind that, ‘This looks great, but I don’t have the money to
invest in this right now. I’ll just take what you’ve taught me, do it, and I’ll
invest next time this offer comes around.’ And you’re right. You’re capable,
you probably can do it alone. But here’s the challenge, I’ve spent the last x
year literally doing just that - doing it on my own. During that time I’ve
spent months floundering around, making mistakes, trying different
things, failing at most of them, and spending tens of thousands along the
way.”
✓ 	“There are lots of things you should do alone. If I’m trying to get to a new
restaurant, I’ll run a Google map and get there myself, I don’t need a
guide. But I can guarantee you, you don’t run a Google map to get to the
top of Mount Everest. For that, you need a guide. Someone who’s been
there, done that, and who will walk you through every step to get there.”
✓ 	“And let’s be real. It’s not like there’s a free, publicly available map that will
get you to this destination. Oh, you could find a few blog posts by people
who claim to know the way. But the Bible says, ‘By their fruits ye shall
know them.’ What’s the probability they’ve actually cracked the code and
CAN guide you to this summit in your life? Probably not.”
✓ 	“I’m giving you a huge opportunity. Yes, you can try to do it alone and pay
the tens of thousands I have, lose months in floundering, and probably
fail” or, “why not just let me help you get there. I give you my word that I
will you get there better, faster, with fewer frustrations, and create even
greater success than you will doing it alone. So why not join me?”
✓ 	Other options:
• 	“People who take action are those who succeed.”
• 	“The internet’s full of bad advice and scam, don’t fall victim to them.”
• 	"Don’t fall into the trap of responding to every opportunity that comes
along - real change happens when you focus.”
• 	"Everything you’ve achieved in your life happened because you took a risk
of some kind.”
✓ 	End emotionally, on a high note that encourages them to take action, now
